title: Laura Puaca
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Laura Puaca
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_titles: Director, Program in Women's and Gender Studies
biography: Laura M. Puaca is a Professor in the History Department at Christopher Newport University. She is also the director of the program in Women, Gender, and Sexuality Studies, as well as the Hampton Roads Oral History Project.
education_entries: Ph D in 20th Cent. U.S. and Women's Studies, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
MA in History, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
BA in History/Women's Studies, Rutgers University
research_interests: Modern U.S. history; women's history; history of education; disability history; history of science and technology; women's and gender studies.
teaching_interests: U.S. history; women's history; gender history; and history of social movements.
publications: Monograph MONOGRAPH: Domesticating Disability: Homemaker Rehabilitation in the Post-World War II U.S. . University of North Carolina Press.
Monograph MONOGRAPH: Domesticating Disability: Homemaker Rehabilitation in the Post-World War II U.S. . University of North Carolina Press.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2020). JOURNAL ARTICLE: “Home Economics, ‘Handicapped Homemakers,’ and Postwar America” . History of Education Quarterly. Volume, 60. Issue, 3. Pages, 380-406.
Monograph MONOGRAPH: Domesticating Disability: Homemaker Rehabilitation in the Post-World War II U.S. . University of North Carolina Press.

Case Study, Academic Journal or Book (2019). "Hicks, Beatrice Alice (2 Jan. 1919–21 Oct. 1979), engineer, inventor, and business executive." . American National Biography, American Council of Learned Societies/Oxford Univer.
Book Review (2018). Women's Work . Science. Volume, Vol. 359. Issue, Issue 6381. Pages, 1221.

Other (2017). History of Science Society Margaret W. Rossiter Prize for Most Outstanding Book on the History of Women in Science Awarded for Searching for Scientific Womanpower: Technocratic Feminism and the Politics of National Security, 1940-1980 .

Other (2017). Disability History Association Publication Award for Best Book Chapter or Article. Awarded for The Largest Occupational Group of All the Disabled: Homemakers with Disabilities and Vocational Rehabilitation in Postwar America in Michael Rembis (ed.), Disabling Domesticity, Palgrave MacMillan. .

Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2017). BOOK CHAPTER: The Largest Occupational Group of All the Disabled: Homemakers with Disabilities and Vocational Rehabilitation in Postwar America . Disabling Domesticity, Palgrave Macmillan.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2016). ARTICLE: How Did the Society of Women Engineers Work to Expand Women's Education and Employment Opportunities (1950-1977)? . Women and Social Movements in the United States, 1600-2000.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2016). EDITED COLLECTION: How Did the Society of Women Engineers Work to Expand Women's Education and Employment Opportunities (1950-1977)? . Women and Social Movements in the United States, 1600-2000.
Monograph (2014). Searching for Scientific Womanpower: Technocratic Feminism and the Politics of National Security, 1940-1980 . University of North Carolina Press.
awards_honors: Schlesinger Library Research Support Grant, Harvard University, Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study. (2025)
Honorable Mention (Second Place), HIstory of Education Article Prize, History of Education Society. (2022)
Alumni Society Award for Excellence in Teaching and Mentoring, Christopher Newport University. (2018)
Zagoren Lecture (Distinguished Alumna lecture), Associate Alumnae of Douglass College. (2018)
Margaret W. Rossiter Prize for outstanding book, History of Science Society. (2017)
Disability History Association Publication Award for Best Article/Chapter, Disability History Association. (2017)
Faculty Excellence Award in Teaching, Christopher Newport University. (2017)

Grants and Sponsored Research: Laura Puaca, "Domesticating Disability: Homemaker Rehabilitation in the Post-World War II U.S.," Sponsored by Harvard University, Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study, Schlesinger Library
